scheme_name: Quality Test Report - Centre for Aromatic Plants
page_title: Quality Test Report - Centre for Aromatic Plants

details: The “Quality Test Report” scheme is implemented by the Centre for Aromatic Plants (CAP), Selaqui, Dehradun, under the Department of Horticulture, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide quality testing facilities at a subsidized rate to registered aromatic farmers, enabling them to obtain authentic quality reports for their produce or essential oils.
benefits: Under this initiative, farmers receive a 50% discount on the quality testing fee for their produce/essential oil. The quality test report enables farmers to obtain a fair market price for their oil.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
The farmer must be engaged in the cultivation of aromatic plants.
The farmer must be registered with the Centre for Aromatic Plants (CAP), Selaqui, Dehradun.

offline: Farmers must submit a sample of the oil they have produced to CAP along with the prescribed fee, after which a quality test report will be issued. It is mandatory for the farmer to visit the CAP office in person to submit the sample.

documents_required: Documents are required as per the guidelines of the Centre for Aromatic Plants (CAP), Selaqui, Dehradun to avail the benefits of the scheme.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Registered aromatic farmers who are residents of Uttarakhand and engaged in aromatic plant cultivation are eligible.

faqs.question: What benefit does the scheme provide to farmers?
faqs.answer: Farmers receive a 50% discount on the quality testing fee for their produce or essential oils.

faqs.question: Why is a quality test report important?
faqs.answer: It helps farmers obtain a fair market price and ensures the quality of their produce is verified scientifically.

faqs.question: How can a farmer avail the scheme?
faqs.answer: Farmers must submit a sample of their produce or oil to CAP along with the prescribed fee.

faqs.question: Is it necessary to visit the CAP office in person?
faqs.answer: Yes, farmers must visit the CAP office in person to submit the sample for testing.
